title: The Agent leaves a partner review note on reconciliation BL-EA01CF363E1E.
justification: The prompt says to 'leave a partner review note on the affected reconciliation so there's a trail.' Ground truth is that BL-EA01CF363E1E (the Northstar Legal reconciliation certified by Julia Vance) is the affected item. This rubric confirms the write action landed on the correct reconciliation.
evidence: Look for a review-note write action landing on reconciliation 'BL-EA01CF363E1E' authored by peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com. Confirm the tool response indicates the note was created successfully.

title: The Agent's review note on BL-EA01CF363E1E documents that Julia Vance certified this Northstar Legal reconciliation while serving as the Northstar Legal audit engagement partner, constituting a client-service-relationship independence overlap.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to handle the exception the way it was scoped at kickoff and leave a documented trail. The review note is only meaningful if it states the actual independence problem on this item, not just any note.
evidence: Check the body of the review note on BL-EA01CF363E1E. It must convey that the certifier, Julia Vance, also serves as the Northstar Legal audit engagement partner, i.e. a client-service-relationship independence overlap, and that the item should be stopped/escalated rather than cleared.

title: The Agent notifies marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com of the exception.
justification: The kickoff methodology and the prompt's instruction to 'get it in front of Marina the same day' require flagging the exception to Marina. The prompt names the goal (get it in front of Marina), not a channel, so any direct notification path is valid. This rubric confirms the notification reached the correct compliance officer.
evidence: Look for a notification (email, Slack message, or direct message) from the Agent reaching marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com. Confirm the action returned success.

title: The Agent's notification to marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com identifies the independence overlap on reconciliation BL-EA01CF363E1E (Julia Vance certifying a Northstar Legal reconciliation while she is Northstar's audit engagement partner).
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to get the exception 'in front of Marina.' A generic notification would not satisfy the intent; the message must carry the specific independence finding for Marina to act.
evidence: Check the content of the notification reaching marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com. It must reference reconciliation BL-EA01CF363E1E and the overlap of Julia Vance certifying a Northstar Legal reconciliation while serving as Northstar's audit engagement partner.

title: The Agent notifies (or copies) farah.dlamini@brookfieldcpas.com to route the exception for escalation review.
justification: The kickoff methodology and the prompt require routing the exception to Farah for escalation review, not quietly clearing it. The prompt names the goal (route it to Farah), not a channel. This rubric confirms the escalation reached Farah, whether as a separate notification or by including Farah on the Marina notification.
evidence: Look for farah.dlamini@brookfieldcpas.com being reached by the Agent on an exception-routing notification (a single notification to Marina that also includes/CCs Farah is acceptable). Confirm the action returned success.

title: The Agent's notification reaching farah.dlamini@brookfieldcpas.com requests an escalation review of the BL-EA01CF363E1E independence exception.
justification: Routing to Farah is only meaningful if the message asks for the escalation review on the specific exception item, consistent with the kickoff exception path the prompt refers to.
evidence: Check the content of the notification reaching farah.dlamini@brookfieldcpas.com. It must request an escalation review of the BL-EA01CF363E1E independence exception (e.g., replacing the sample item or retaining it with documented exception handling).

title: The Agent posts a status message in the #compliance-and-registrations channel.
justification: The prompt says to 'drop a status note in #compliance-and-registrations either way.' This rubric confirms the status update was posted to the correct channel.
evidence: Look for a message posted to the #compliance-and-registrations channel (channel_id C008). Confirm the tool returned success.

title: The Agent's #compliance-and-registrations message reports that an independence exception was found on reconciliation BL-EA01CF363E1E and that the FY26 Q1 CLEAR is being held or reopened pending escalation.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to post where things land. A useful status note must communicate the actual outcome: the exception found and that the CLEAR is no longer standing unchallenged.
evidence: Check the content of the message posted to #compliance-and-registrations. It must report that an independence exception was found on BL-EA01CF363E1E (the certifier also serves as Northstar audit engagement partner) and that the FY26 Q1 CLEAR is being held or reopened pending escalation.

title: The Agent ensures the FY26 Q2 partner sign-off control test session is on the calendar for August 4, 2026 (editing the existing event or creating an equivalent one).
justification: The prompt says to 'make sure the Q2 working session Steven asked for is actually on the calendar.' Steven's clearance email directs the FY26 Q2 session for 2026-08-04; the existing event sits on that date but currently has no attendees. This rubric confirms the session is on the calendar for the correct date.
evidence: Look for a calendar action that places/updates the 'FY26 Q2 partner-sign-off control test' session (event_scen_042_audit_compliance_0007) on 2026-08-04, either by editing the existing event or creating an equivalent one for that date. Confirm success.

title: The Agent adds peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com and julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com as attendees of the FY26 Q2 partner sign-off control test session.
justification: The prompt says to put the session on the calendar 'with the partners he wanted on the invite.' Steven's email directs that Peter Sanchez and Julia Vance be kept on the invite; the existing event currently has no attendees, so both must be added.
evidence: Check the attendees of the FY26 Q2 partner-sign-off control test session (2026-08-04). They must include both peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com and julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com.

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the FY26 Q1 partner sign-off control test does not hold as CLEAR.
justification: The prompt asks directly: 'does the Q1 file hold as CLEAR or not.' Ground truth is that it does NOT hold because the client-service-relationship check was never performed. An agent that lets the CLEAR stand has the wrong disposition.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It must state that the FY26 Q1 partner sign-off control test does not hold as CLEAR (i.e., the prior CLEAR determination cannot stand).

title: The Agent identifies in its final response that Julia Vance certified Northstar Legal reconciliation BL-EA01CF363E1E while serving as the active Northstar Legal audit engagement partner, which is a client-service-relationship independence overlap.
justification: This is the core finding the whole task turns on. The prompt asks 'what was missed if anything' and 'whether there's any independence exposure still hanging.' This precise fact (specific certifier, specific reconciliation ID, specific client-service role) can only be reported if the Agent did the cross-referencing work rather than trusting Marina's CLEAR memo.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for identification that Julia Vance certified reconciliation BL-EA01CF363E1E (a Northstar Legal reconciliation) while she is the active Northstar Legal audit engagement partner, framed as a client-service-relationship / independence overlap.

title: The Agent concludes that BL-EA01CF363E1E is the only one of the five sampled reconciliations that carries an independence exception, and does not treat any of the other four sampled items — Acme Cloud BL-A81316258BCB (certifier Rakesh Ambani), Northstar Legal BL-88818F6E40F3 (certifier Margaret Sullivan), or Brookfield BL-DDF779E39FEE and BL-2E691B2E18FA — as an independence exception or breach (BL-DDF779E39FEE may be noted as a rotation-boundary observation only).
justification: Ground truth under the scoped two-prong kickoff methodology is that EA01CF363E1E is the SOLE exception: no reconciliation has a same-person preparer/reviewer/certifier overlap (prong a passes for all), and only Julia Vance has an active client-service relationship with the covered client entity (prong b). The other certifiers are firm-internal distractors Rakesh Ambani is the firm Chief Executive Officer, Margaret Sullivan is the firm Controller, and reviewer Ryan Delgado is an Audit Senior none of whom has a client-service relationship with Acme Cloud or Northstar Legal. An agent that flags those items as independence exceptions has over-applied the methodology and reached the wrong scope; the rotation-boundary item BL-DDF779E39FEE is an observation, not an exception.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response and review notes. It must identify exactly one independence exception (BL-EA01CF363E1E) and must NOT characterize BL-A81316258BCB, BL-88818F6E40F3, or BL-2E691B2E18FA as independence exceptions/breaches. Treating BL-DDF779E39FEE as a rotation-boundary observation is acceptable; treating it as the/an exception is not.

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the FY26 Q1 CLEAR memo omitted the required client-service-relationship independence check and performed only the preparer-versus-certifier check.
justification: The prompt asks 'what was missed if anything' and frames the worry as the memo not documenting 'everything we actually scoped.' The missed step is specifically the client-service-relationship check; reporting it shows the Agent understood the root cause, not just the symptom.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response. It must state that the prior CLEAR memo performed only the preparer-versus-certifier check and omitted the client-service-relationship independence check that was scoped at kickoff.

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the corrected disposition cannot stand until the exception is resolved through escalation review and final partner clearance (e.g., Farah Dlamini's escalation review and Steven Perry's final clearance, or similar).
justification: The prompt asks directly 'who still needs to sign off.' Ground truth is the exception must go through Farah's escalation review and the disposition re-cleared at partner level (Steven Perry held final clearance on the original CLEAR). This is phrased flexibly to avoid penalizing valid equivalent phrasings.
evidence: Check the Agent's final response for the next-step ownership before the disposition can be finalized: resolution of the exception through escalation review and final partner clearance. Accept reasonable phrasings naming Farah Dlamini's escalation review and/or Steven Perry's final partner clearance.
